channel.title: Comments for page "The blunders are all there on the board, waiting to be made."
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The blunders are all there on the board, waiting to be made."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's okay, but it doesn't really flow properly. There's a few places where it's got a nice meter(?) and then there's an awkward sentence and it falls over.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Came here from the YouTube reading to tell that I enjoy this tale very much.

The part I find most interesting is the idea that 343, 2343 and his wife are all manifestation of the same being.

Also, putting two scips together draws attention to their articles in a way - and it is hard not to notice how the wiki has evolved. God of series I basically is not contained, while God in series III IS and his containment has redundancies and stuff even though the reader doesn't even see his powers to the extent of 343.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems you have fallen for one of the classic blunders +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At this rate, with the amount of your tales involving 343, we might ad well have a tag for him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I had to look up 2343.

A fun tale, with a natural flow and escalation.

A small add on to one of the sentences referencing 2343’s smoking habit could be a nice little detail.

Maybe: SCP-2343 looked down at the board. "Why do we keep doing this?" he said, extinguishing his cigarette.

Just a thought.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Title is a quote from Savielly Tartakower. Just a little story I thought up last night and thought was worth a post.

Page image is from here: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Paris_-_Playing_chess_at_the_Jardins_du_Luxembourg_-_2966.jpg
